meaning: to be scary; to be scared

expressions.english: scary dream
expressions.notes: 꿈 = dream

expressions.english: scary movie
expressions.notes: 영화 = movie

expressions.english: scary story
expressions.notes: 이야기 = story; what one says

expressions.english: to have a scary dream
expressions.notes: 꿈을 꾸다 = to have a dream; to dream

expressions.english: to tell a scary story
expressions.notes: 이야기를 하다 = to talk, speak, say, tell; to have a conversation; to tell a story

expressions.english:
Aren't you scared?
Isn't it scary?
expressions.notes: 안 = not

expressions.english:
It's not scary at all.
I'm not scared at all.
expressions.notes: 하나도 = not even one; not at all

expressions.english: Don't be afraid.
expressions.notes:
무서워하다 = to be afraid; to fear
-지 마세요 = (polite) Don't + V

expressions.english: Don't pretend you are not scared.
expressions.notes:
- 척 하다 = to pretend
-지 말아요. = Don't + V.

expressions.english: The movie I saw yesterday was really scary.
expressions.notes:
어제 = yesterday
보다 = to see; to look; to watch; to meet up; to read
정말 = really; very

expressions.english: When I'm at home alone, it's really scary.
expressions.notes:
집 = house, home
혼자 = alone, by oneself
있다 = to be there; to exist; to have
-(으)면 = if + S + V; when/once + S + V

expressions.english: Do you like watching scary movies?
expressions.notes: 좋아하다 = to like

expressions.korean: 저는 무서운 놀이기구는 잘 못 타요.
expressions.english: I can't ride scary theme park rides very well.
expressions.notes:
저 = (polite) I; me
못 = can't
놀이기구 = amusement park rides
잘 = well; carefully
타다 = to ride; to get on; to take (vehicle)

expressions.korean: 이 영화 별로 안 무서우니까 걱정하지 마세요.
expressions.english: This movie is not that scary, so don't worry.
expressions.notes:
이 = this
별로 = not particularly, not very, not so much, not really
-(으)니까 = since, because
걱정하다 = to worry

expressions.korean: 어제 무서운 영화를 봐서 밤에 무서운 꿈을 꿨어요.
expressions.english: I watched a scary movie yesterday, so I had a scary dream at night.
expressions.notes: 밤 = night

expressions.korean: 속으로는 무서웠지만 겉으로는 안 무서운 척 했어요.
expressions.english: I was scared on the inside, but I pretended that I wasn't scared.
expressions.notes:
속 = inside
-지만 = but, however; though
겉 = outside, exterior

expressions.korean: 옆집에 큰 개가 있는데 지나갈 때마다 너무 무서워요.
expressions.english: There is a huge dog next door that scares me every time I pass by it.
expressions.notes:
옆 = next
크다 = to be big; to be tall; to be loud
큰 is the adjective form of 크다.
개 = dog
지나가다 = to pass by, go past
-(으)ㄹ = whenever + S + V
너무 = too much, excessively; very

expressions.korean: 친구한테 무서운 이야기를 들어서 밤에 잠을 못 잤어요.
expressions.english: I heard a scary story from my friend, so I couldn't sleep at night.
expressions.notes:
듣다 = to hear; to listen
잠 = sleep
잠을 자다 = to sleep

expressions.korean: 그 선생님은 평소에는 온화하신데 화가 나면 정말 무서우세요.
expressions.english: The teacher is usually mild-tempered, but when he gets mad, he's really scary.
expressions.notes:
그 = the; that
선생님 = teacher
평소 = usual day, ordinary day
온화하다 = to be mild-tempered; to be gentle, mild-mannered
화 = anger
화가 나다 = to get angry/upset

expressions.korean: 어제 택시를 탔는데 기사 아저씨가 너무 빨리 운전해서 무서웠어요.
expressions.english: I took a taxi yesterday, but the driver drove too fast, so I was scared.
expressions.notes:
택시 = cab, taxi
기사 아저씨 = male driver (mainly of a bus or a taxi)
빨리다 = to be fast
빨리 is an adverb form of 빨라다.
운전하다 = to drive
-아/어/여서 = because, since
